Ad Text:
This role supports a major government upgrade from SAP ECC6 to S/4HANA by analysing finance processes, defining requirements, and ensuring successful design, testing, and data migration activities. It works closely with finance SMEs, change leads, and project teams to deliver compliant, efficient, and future‑ready financial capabilities

Client Details

Our client is a well-established public sector organisation operating in the technology space. As a medium-sized entity, they are committed to driving innovation and efficiency in their operations to serve the community effectively.

Description

As the Business Analyst, you will:

Analyse as‑is finance processes in SAP ECC6 and support the design of future‑state processes in S/4HANA.
Assist with configuration reviews, business process re‑engineering and finance data mapping.
Support data migration planning, including cleansing, validation and reconciliation.
Prepare and review documentation: process models, business requirements, functional specifications and user stories.
Participate in solution design workshops and ensure finance needs are fully represented.
Develop test plans, coordinate and support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and manage finance‑related defects.
Contribute to training materials, change initiatives and go‑live readiness activities.
Maintain clear, auditable project documentation aligned with government standards.
Identify and escalate risks, issues and dependencies as needed.

Profile

A successful Business Analyst should have:

Strong understanding of government finance processes (AP/AR, assets, financial reporting, controls).
Demonstrated experience with SAP FI/CO in ECC6 and awareness of S/4HANA capabilities (e.g., Universal Journal, Fiori apps).
Experience on large‑scale SAP or finance transformation programs.
Excellent analytical skills and the ability to translate business needs into system requirements.
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills across finance, ICT and project teams.
Experience with BPMN process mapping, Visio, Excel, JIRA and Confluence.
Commitment to a safe, respectful and inclusive work environment.

Job Offer

Competitive hourly rate of AUD 100.0 - AUD 110.0 per hour + super.
Opportunity to work within a reputable public sector organisation in Brisbane City.
Chance to contribute to impactful technology projects.
Temporary contract offering flexibility and valuable experience.

This is a fantastic opportunity for a Business Analyst looking to make a difference within the public sector. If this sounds like the role for you, we encourage you to apply today or reach out to Dev Choudhary on 0466379111 or devchoudhary@michaelpage.com.au

Job Title: Pre-Sales Commercial Finance Analyst – Mining Technology/Services
Ad Text:
Own and elevate the commercial value narrative of a scaling business 
Partner directly with a high-calibre CFO and executive team 
Lead the shift from transactional pricing to outcome-based value 
About the role: 
This is a newly created mandate at the centre of commercial strategy. Our client has built a strong operational platform. The next phase is sharpening how that operational performance is translated into clear, defensible financial value. 
Reporting to the CFO and working closely with Sales and Operations, you will lead the development and refinement of structured financial models that quantify real commercial impact. You will interrogate operational data, stress-test assumptions and build finance-grade business cases that withstand scrutiny from sophisticated client finance teams. 

Your leadership will be defined by rigour and influence. You will formalize modelling standards, embed consistency across customer engagements and strengthen commercial positioning in market. Success looks like this: quantified value becomes central to every major conversation, pricing frameworks mature beyond product or service transactions, and the executive team relies on your logic to guide commercial decisions. 
This is not a passive analytical role. It is a visible commercial leadership position anchored in financial discipline. 

About You 
You have built your foundation in management or financial accounting within a mining or asset-intensive environment. You understand mine economics, cost drivers and the operational levers that influence margin and performance. Hard rock exposure across processing, production, maintenance and tailings environments will resonate strongly. Equally, your experience may sit within heavy industry, energy, large-scale manufacturing or infrastructure, where you have partnered closely with operations and translated complex production inputs into clear commercial insight. 
 
You bring advanced Excel modelling capability and the ability to build models from first principles, not simply maintain them. You are comfortable translating operational inputs into commercial insight and challenging assumptions constructively. 
You combine technical accounting depth with commercial judgement. You can sit with engineers and operators, then confidently defend your modelling logic with senior finance stakeholders. You are curious, analytical and tenacious, willing to work through imperfect data to reach robust conclusions.  
 
What’s on Offer: 
You will join a PE-backed, growth-oriented organisation with global ambition and a clear innovation agenda. The leadership team is accessible, commercially astute and committed to building capability, not hierarchy. 
You will have direct executive access, genuine influence over strategic direction, cross-functional exposure across finance, operations and sales, and the opportunity to build a capability from the ground up. You will operate in a culture that values ownership, intellectual honesty and performance. If you are ready to step into a role where your thinking changes the commercial trajectory of a business, this is the opportunity to lead that transformation. 

As part of the recruitment process, reference checks will be conducted, with two recent references from direct line managers being mandatory. In addition, employers may require other pre-employment checks at their discretion, which can include medical, police, or psychometric assessments depending on the specific requirements of the role.  
 
For further information please apply via the role advert or contact Peter Gwizdalla or Tiffany Cording with any specific questions: tiffanyc@areteexecutive.com.au or 0450 277 192. 
 
Arete Executive specialises in the Search & Recruitment of Leadership & Executive roles throughout Australia. We offer cost effective headhunting services from 10% with guaranteed results delivered in under 20 days. Candidate confidentiality is assured with all applications, with your privacy being our utmost priority. Partner with us for fast, reliable, and impactful recruitment solutions.

6 month role
Brisbane CBD based
Our Queensland Government client is undertaking major business readiness project in conjunction with the Shared Service Provider to upgrade the finance system from SAP ECC6 to S/4HANA.

Closing Date: ASAP

For more information, please contact Angela Anderson at angela.a@edenritchie.com.au

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to join the client's project delivery team and play a key role in a major enterprise transformation initiative involving the migration of financial systems to SAP S/4HANA. You will work alongside experienced project leaders and technical specialists to deliver a critical data migration program that supports improved financial management and digital capability. The role offers the chance to contribute to large-scale system modernisation, influence key project outcomes, and collaborate with stakeholders across multiple teams to ensure a successful transition to a modern SAP platform.

Initial 8 month contract with scope to extend. Must be able to attend the office in Brisbane CBD 3 days per week. Unfortunately sponosorship is not available for this opportunity.


The Role

As a Data Migration Lead you will be responsible for leading the data migration activities associated with a major SAP S/4HANA implementation, ensuring financial data is accurately analysed, transformed and migrated to support business needs.

Your other responsibilities will include:

Partner with the Project Manager to deliver data migration outcomes aligned to project objectives and business requirements
Analyse financial transactions and provide recommendations to executive stakeholders based on defined business attribute requirements
Engage with stakeholders across the organisation to gather, clarify and formalise data migration requirements
Lead the design and development of data migration plans including data extraction, transformation and loading into SAP S/4HANA
Oversee reconciliation processes, identify and resolve data defects, and ensure data quality during migration activities
Coordinate migration trials and support the business cutover into the production environment for go-live


About You
In order to be a successful applicant you will demonstrate strong experience delivering complex data migration initiatives within financial systems environments.

You will also have:

Proven experience leading data migration activities for SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC6 implementations or upgrades, particularly within financial management systems
Demonstrated experience conducting complex systems analysis, data migration, testing and production cutover activities for large enterprise systems
Strong ability to analyse financial data and identify opportunities to improve business processes and operational efficiency
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to build collaborative relationships across technical teams and executive stakeholders
High-level communication, consultation and negotiation skills when working across business and technology teams
Understanding of financial system integrations, reconciliation processes, internal controls and audit requirements



Benefits

Initial 8 month contract with scope to extend
Flexible working arrangements, 2 days work from home per week
Opportunity to contribute to a major digital transformation program
Collaborative and supportive team culture
Meaningful work delivering improved services and outcomes for stakeholders

The Security Business Analyst is a specialist role responsible for accelerating the onboarding of enterprise applications and platforms into the organisation's Splunk Enterprise Security (SIEM) and enabling automated alerting for the SOC. The role addresses a key constraint by taking on the discovery, definition, and documentation of log sources and security monitoring requirements-work that security engineers cannot complete at scale.

By working directly with application, platform, and service owners, the Security Business Analyst identifies security‑relevant logging, defines monitoring and detection requirements, and produces clear artefacts for Security Operations and SIEM engineering. Through strong business analysis and security expertise, the role ensures consistent, scalable, and risk‑aligned security monitoring across SaaS, cloud, and enterprise environments.



Responsibilities


Engage system owners to understand architectures, data flows, and logging.
Identify, analyse, and document security‑relevant log sources.
Maintain a central log source register with owners, access methods, and retention.
Prioritise log sources with Security Engineering and SOC teams.
Develop security stories and use cases for monitoring and detection.
Define incident‑response context and key escalation contacts.
Build strong relationships across IT, Security, and business teams.
Communicate progress, risks, and dependencies clearly.
Keep security processes and documentation up to date.



Skills Summary


Experience with cybersecurity, Security Operations, SIEM, and logging platforms
Strong understanding of authentication, authorisation, audit logging, and incident response
Experience working in regulated, risk‑aware, or compliance‑driven environments
High proficiency with Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word, Visio, etc.)
Cybersecurity certifications (e.g., CISSP, CREST, GIAC, OSCP) will be highly regarded
Experience working in ITIL‑aligned environments
Familiarity with a broad range of enterprise and SaaS platforms
Ability to work closely with Cyber Security leadership, Security Operations, and Engineering teams
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Mars: Where your Tomorrow Starts Today! 

Mars is a family-owned business that has been making products for people and their pets for more than 100 years. We’re behind some of the best loved brands in the world including MARS®, SNICKERS®, EXTRA®, ECLIPSE®, PEDIGREE®, WHISKAS®, DOLMIO®, BEN’S ORIGINAL ® MASTERFOODS ® and ROYAL CANIN® and we are dedicated to making a positive impact on people and the planet.  

 

About Mars Food & Nutrition Australia

Mars Food & Nutrition is dedicated to providing better food today and a better world tomorrow. For us, food is more than just what’s on our plate. We know that what we eat, where it comes from and who we share it with matters. We believe that a better world tomorrow is one where more people have access to healthy meals; more people cook and sit down together to enjoy shared dinnertimes; and more food can be produced with less.
 
We have an ambition to enable delicious, healthier eating to be part of consumers’ daily lives. Our portfolio of much-loved brands includes MASTERFOODS™, DOLMIO™, BEN'S ORIGINAL™, KAN TONG®, and PROMITE®.

 

About the opportunity

Reporting to the Marketing Director and as a member of the Marketing Leadership Team, this influential role will provide data-driven insights that guide brand, category, and consumer strategy.

You will help unlock the full potential of our brands by opening new creative spaces and focusing the business on the growth opportunities that matter most. You will turn consumer, shopper, and market data into actionable recommendations, and empower teams across Marketing, Sales, and Category to drive timely, impactful decisions.

You will help Mars F&N Australia anticipate trends, inform innovation, and drive growth across MasterFoods and other strategic brand portfolios.

Please note, this is a Sydney based role which requires travel to our Wyong site.

 

What you’ll do

Lead consumer, shopper, and portfolio understanding to support brand positioning, integrated brand experiences, and innovation development.

Turn complex data into actionable insights, driving growth initiatives and portfolio strategy.

Inspire and provoke the business to act on insights with speed, turning analysis into commercially successful propositions.

Partner with internal teams (Marketing, Sales, R&D, S&F) and external partners (agencies, data providers like IRI) to deliver high-impact insights.

Shape the Australian insight agenda, prioritising research, foresight, and co-funded growth initiatives aligned with regional and global strategies.

Oversee adoption of data analytics tools, AI-driven insights, and self-service dashboards to drive data-led decision-making.

Embed a continuous Growth Process across the business, connecting insights to strategy and execution.

 

What we are looking for:

An experienced CMI professional, you combine analytical rigour with strategic thinking and strong commercial curiosity. You bring a passion for understanding consumers and a proven ability to translate insight into business impact. In addition, you’ll have:

A degree in Market Research, Marketing, Business, or a related field.

Deep expertise in category growth, brand strategy, and consumer and shopper behaviour.

Strong quantitative and qualitative research capability, including ad hoc and syndicated data.

A track record of turning complex data into clear, actionable insights that shape strategy and decision-making.

Strategic thinker with strong commercial acumen and a passion for understanding consumers.

Well-developed influencing and communication skills, with the ability to engage and inspire senior stakeholders.

A proactive, agile mindset and confidence making decisions in ambiguous or data-light environments.

Proficiency in data analytics tools and platforms (e.g. IRI), with awareness of emerging AI and digital research solutions.

 

What can you expect from Mars?

Mars will provide you with genuine career progression focusing on you and your career goals. Best-in-class learning and development support from day one, including access to our in-house Mars University. A global health and wellbeing program to support flexibility and work-life balance, with the opportunity to make a difference with our volunteer and ambassador programs.

Be part of a company that is contributing to making the world a better place today and every day through on our sustainable generation plan. A culture that offers collaboration, leadership and rewards you for the role you play within Mars.

There’s never been a more exciting time to shape the energy transition. As a Digital Innovation Engineer, with a strong software and analytics stack, you’ll play a pivotal role in transforming how an electricity distribution business operates by designing, building and delivering scalable, data-driven solutions that improve safety, performance and customer outcomes.

This role will require you to work closely with different teams across Endeavour Energy as we work together to deliver safe, reliable and affordable power to our customers.

About the Role

This is a highly technical, hands-on role for an engineer who thrives on solving complex operational and business challenges but also enjoys working closely with business stakeholders. You’ll identify inefficiencies, uncover data gaps and build end-to-end digital solutions that automate processes, enhance decision-making and unlock measurable value across the organisation.

From advanced analytics, python scripting, AI/ML to digital twins and automation, you’ll apply modern technologies to real-world engineering problems and see your solutions in action.

What You’ll Do

Analyse operational, engineering and business processes to identify improvement opportunities and define clear technical problem statements
Design and deliver end-to-end digital solutions from data ingestion and transformation through to analytics, automation and visualisation
Build dashboards, analytics tools and decision-support systems that drive operational performance, safety and productivity
Develop automation solutions to eliminate manual processes and improve data quality and workflow reliability
Apply emerging technologies (Digital Twins, AI/ML, advanced analytics, robotics) to practical engineering challenges
Apply advanced programming and Python skills to analyse LiDAR and imagery datasets, delivering high-quality spatial insights to support engineering outcomes
Think outside the box with innovative and creative solutions as well as strong problem solving skills
Partner with stakeholders to understand requirements, validate assumptions and iteratively deliver business value
Continuously improve digital products based on performance insights and user feedback
Establish reusable tools, standards and patterns to uplift digital capability across the organisation
Stay at the forefront of emerging technologies and industry innovation

About You

You’re analytical, commercially aware and naturally curious. You enjoy breaking down complex problems, working across disciplines and influencing others to embrace smarter ways of working.

You bring:

3–10 years’ experience in a similar professional or technical role
A Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Data Science or a related discipline

Technical capability in:

SQL, Python, Azure Analytics, Databricks
Power BI, Knime, FME or similar analytics platforms
Data modelling, automation and workflow design

You’ll also demonstrate:

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, applying innovative solutions
The ability to manage multiple concurrent projects
Clear and persuasive communication skills to handle stakeholder management
Commercial awareness and understanding of enterprise impacts
A solid understanding of electricity/energy utility operations

Why Join Us?

Work on meaningful projects that power communities
Deliver innovative solutions with real operational impact
Collaborate with engineers, analysts and business leaders
Help shape the digital future of energy

If you’re passionate about combining engineering, data and innovation to solve real-world problems — we’d love to hear from you.

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - Finance Transformation (6m FTC)
Location: North West & Hills District, Sydney NSW (Hybrid)
Ad Text:
6-month contract with high possibility for extension - Immediate start ideal
Reputable industrial services organisation
North West Sydney, hybrid working & onsite parking available.
Following recent transformation initiatives within this multi-entity industrial organisation, an opportunity has arisen for a Senior Finance Business Analyst to drive finance process standardisation. Operating at the intersection of Finance process design and ERP capability, you will ensure workflows are aligned to best practice, efficiently enabled by systems, and scalable across the group.
Key Responsibilities

Key responsibilities 

Analyse end-to-end finance workflows and harmonise processes across Record-to-Report, Procure-to-Pay (P2P), Order-to-Cash (O2C), Payroll and Shared Services
Identify inefficiencies, duplication and inconsistent practices across entities,  and align them to current ERP functionality
Develop practical documentation including process flows, SOPs, ownership matrices and control frameworks
Contribute to shaping and sequencing process improvements alongside system enablement
Identify interdependencies across data, configuration, training and behavioural change
Facilitate cross-functional workshops to align stakeholders on standard outcomes, clearly communicate change implications, risks and trade-offs
Review and update finance policies to reflect agreed processes and system usage, ensuring documented procedures align with governance, compliance and audit requirements
  
Ideal Profile 

Minimum 6 years' experience with finance process improvement experience (Accounting, Shared Services, FP&A or similar)
Proven ERP experience (INFOR LN highly regarded; other large ERP systems including SAP welcome)
Demonstrated capability in process design and standardisation
Experience mapping business workflows to system capability
Strong documentation, facilitation and stakeholder engagement skills
Experience in finance transformation or multi-entity environments will be highly regarded.
  
How to Apply:
To apply, please click on the appropriate link below. Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Samantha Tan at stan@sharpandcarter.com.au.

Job Title: Head of Data & Engineering | Murarrie | Hybrid
Ad Text:
The Company

Robert Half are partnering with Goodstart Early Learning, Australia's largest not-for-profit early learning providers, with their centre support office located in Brisbane. Goodstart is purpose-driven social enterprise, committed to improving outcomes for children, families, and communities across the country.

As part of Goodstart's Centre Support Team, you'll be at the core of this mission, supporting Goodstart centre teams to create impactful, high-quality early learning experiences. Your work will help shape the future, ensuring every child gets the best start in life.

As part of an ongoing technology and data transformation, Goodstart are strengthening their enterprise data capability to support evidence-based decision making and maximise community impact, all within a collaborative, values-driven culture.

The Role

This is a highly influential leadership opportunity with significant impact across the organisation. Reporting directly to the CIO, you will partner with senior business and technology stakeholders to shape and deliver Goodstart's enterprise data strategy. Note: this role is internally titled 'ICT Manager - Data Engineering & Experience'

The position leads Goodstart's enterprise data capability across data engineering, analytics enablement, and data experience, owning both the strategic roadmap and the evolution of how data is leveraged across the organisation. While demonstrated technical credibility is essential, strong people and organisational leadership is the priority, focused on enabling high-performing teams and sustainable delivery outcomes.

You will oversee multiple data streams, ensuring initiatives are aligned to organisational priorities, effectively governed, and deliver meaningful insights that support business decision-making.

Responsibilities:

Lead and develop a high-performing data division across engineering, analytics enablement, and data experience, including 4 direct reports who each manage a core data stream
Define and deliver the enterprise roadmap for Data Engineering and Experience, ensuring alignment with organisational priorities
Drive the advancement of a contemporary cloud-based data infrastructure and build efficient, modern data pipelines
Work with key business and technical stakeholders to design and evolve a scalable, modular data architecture
Collaborate with the data governance function to maintain data consistency, security, and compliance with relevant regulations
Provide technical and people leadership across agile delivery teams
Enable business insights and efficiencies through strategic use of data and analytics

Requirements:

Experience in a similar senior leadership role within the data management field
Extensive experience managing data infrastructures and data teams
Experience guiding organisations through modern data platform or cloud transformation journeys (Microsoft Azure / Fabric experience highly regarded)
Experience balancing competing priorities and implementing governance or demand management frameworks
Data architecture and project management experience
Degree in Computer Science, Data Management, IT, or a related field

Apply Today

Goodstart is an equal opportunity employer that is proud of their inclusive and diverse work environment. We know that Goodstarters from diverse cultures, backgrounds and experiences strengthen our teams and help us grow. Goodstart is also deeply committed to Reconciliation and creating an environment where Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander People feel a strong sense of belonging and connection.

Safety is Goodstart priority and we will take any action necessary to ensure that all children can feel safe and be safe, and are safeguarded from abuse, neglect and harm. As a Child Safe Organisation, all of our candidates must have or obtain a valid working with children check.

Learn more about our Brisbane recruitment services: https://www.roberthalf.com.au/brisbane

By clicking 'apply', you give your express consent that Robert Half may use your personal information to process your job application and to contact you from time to time for future employment opportunities. For further information on how Robert Half processes your personal information and how to access and correct your information, please read the Robert Half privacy notice: https://www.roberthalf.com/au/en/privacy. Please do not submit any sensitive personal data to us in your resume (such as government ID numbers, ethnicity, gender, religion, marital status or trade union membership) as we do not collect your sensitive personal data at this time.

Through the merger of Bank Australia and Qudos Bank we’re creating a better, stronger and more resilient bank for our customers and employees. As one company with two trusted brands, we’re building on the strong foundations of our history and shared values as we bring together the best of both banks.

Following merger Day 1, the Bank has embarked on an ambitious post-merger integration program to set the conditions for future M&A growth as soon as possible. This rapid growth means the bank has also reached a scale and complexity that requires concurrent transition to a Significant Financial Institution Control Environment in 2026.

We are looking for a strategically-minded and energetic Portfolio Analysis Lead to lead the Bank’s emerging business analysis capability and support the governance of the organisation’s Strategic Project Portfolio while assisting with solving complex business problems, scoping the project work required to realise the Corporate Strategy, delivering assigned work with limited supervision and setting an example in the art of the possible.

The Portfolio Analysis Lead is part of a team that converts business intent into reality. The Portfolio Analysis Lead provides meaningful input into the design and development of high-profile and high-risk activities that progress the Corporate Strategy. Their insights are drawn from advisory and delivery experience as well as situations where they have been required to achieve desired outcomes individually, as part of a team or by leading a team.

The Portfolio Analysis Lead is a leader who knows their team and advises on the deployment of their people in a manner that sets them up for success while considering the needs of the Bank alongside the needs of the individual. They are also someone who takes ownership of what needs to be done and does what work is required to get it done.

This role can be based out of either the Head Office in Sydney (Mascot) or Melbourne (Collingwood); however, some travel between these locations will be required.   

Why join us

We have big plans to become Australia’s leading purpose-driven bank. You will be joining an emerging capability within a growing organisation that is in transition to being designated as a Significant Financial Institution by the Regulator.
As a certified B Corp, we’re part of a global business community who meet the highest social and environmental standards, balancing profit and purpose.
Every role and person in our bank is essential to bringing our values, purpose and aspiration to life.
We offer flexible working options, 14% super and a bonus leave scheme.
Our staff have access to a range of meaningful benefits to support their physical and mental wellbeing including 24/7 free counselling, free flu vaccinations and discounted gym memberships.
We also support staff with study assistance, paid parental leave (regardless of gender), volunteer, bereavement, gender affirmation and family violence leave; and the opportunity for bonus annual leave.
We support our staff to grow their careers through learning and development and an organisational culture where we reward and recognise innovation and values.

What you will be doing

Lead a small team of business analysts in support of strategic M&A, Regulatory, IT and business Transformation projects that are designed to realise the Bank’s Corporate Strategy.
Assist the Enterprise Portfolio Manager in their efforts to support the development, implementation and evolution of the Bank's Strategic Project Portfolio over a rolling 18-month time horizon.
Partner with Executives and senior leaders to support the governance of the Strategic Project Portfolio across the enterprise and support timely decision making.
Partner with the Office of the CEO to ensure alignment of project delivery and the associated resource allocation with strategic priorities. This extends to interfacing with aspects of discovery and delivery handoffs.
Provide thought leadership and set the example for business and portfolio analysis.
Lead, or support as directed, multiple activities across the Bank’s Strategic Project Portfolio, applying the right level of management or coordination to empower business leaders to achieve timely outcomes with an appropriate level of governance oversight.
Bring the right minds together to identify risks, resolve issues, solve complex problems and scope the work required to deliver the Strategic Project Portfolio.
Apply assigned frameworks and disciplines to assist the Bank deliver its Strategic Project Portfolio, protect its critical path timeline and realise business objectives.
Take ownership of what needs to be done, and do the work to get it done. 
Oversee and/or deliver gap analyses, scoping studies, concept papers, business cases, requirements documentation, work breakdown structures, options papers, and other artefacts supporting major business decisions.

What you will bring

Demonstrated success in delivering material outcomes within strategic transformation programs.
Proven ability to solve problems by leveraging the collective expertise of a cross functional panel of senior leaders and/or subject matter experts.
Proven ability to engage, advise, and influence senior leaders and dispersed teams across hybrid environments.
Commitment to Bank Australia’s purpose, aspiration, values and brand as well as the B Corp ethos.
Tertiary qualification in Business Analysis, Commerce, Finance, Technology or related field of study.
Strong experience in a similar role or capacity.

Even better, if you have

Pre or post Day 1 M&A delivery experience within the context of a previous Mutual Bank merger or another commercial acquisition or separation is highly desirable.
Experience supporting the consolidation and data migration of two separate core banking systems into one enterprise core banking system is desirable. 
Experience supporting the implementation of a strategic enterprise-wide technology platform of material scale and cost is desirable.
Experience supporting a financial institution comply with heightened regulatory requirements is desirable.
Experience delivering client engagements in a Tier 1 consulting environment is highly desirable.

By submitting an application, you agree to Bank Australia’s Privacy Policy for applicants and confirm that you are legally able to work in Australia. Bank Australia is an equal opportunity employer committed to sustainable development. We encourage people from different backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people from different cultural backgrounds and people with disabilities because we want to reflect the diversity of our communities. We are committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment process and supportive workplace.

Bank Australia reserves the right to withdraw this advertisement at any time. Candidates will be progressed as applications are received, rather than after the advertisement closure date. If you wish to be considered, please submit your application as soon as possible. Please note, the successful candidate will be subject to satisfactory background checks including but not limited to police and previous employment.

Please note that candidates who do not have full working rights in Australia will not be considered.

Job Title: Program Manager - Data Governance & IT Uplift
Ad Text:
A large organisation is embarking on a significant multi‑year transformation focused on lifting its data governance capability and modernising data platforms. This program spans technology, governance, domain delivery and enterprise‑wide alignment.




The role requires a senior Program Manager who can bring order, clarity and strategic direction across a set of interconnected workstreams. This includes shaping the program plan, maintaining flow across teams, and ensuring the initiative progresses cohesively rather than as isolated efforts.


Key Requirements
The ideal candidate will be an experienced program leader with a strong background in data or digital transformation. Key capabilities include:


Experience managing large‑scale data, analytics or platform uplift initiatives.
Strong understanding of how major data investments are sequenced and how benefits are realised.
Ability to coordinate delivery across governance, engineering, architecture, security, procurement and finance.
Skilled at simplifying complex program information for senior audiences.
Experience managing interdependencies, risks and competing priorities across multiple streams.
Strong financial literacy, including forecasting, investment tracking and assessing sequencing impacts.
Confident working across early shaping stages through to delivery execution.
Key Responsibilities
In this role, the Program Manager will:


Translate a high‑level roadmap into a structured program plan with clear phases and interdependencies.
Ensure delivery streams remain aligned to the broader program direction.
Manage program‑level risks, issues and dependencies and escalate where needed.
Oversee financial tracking and ensure investment planning remains aligned with program sequencing.
Support measurement and tracking of expected benefits.
Produce consolidated reporting for executive and portfolio governance.
Represent the program in broader portfolio and steering forums.
Work closely with delivery and domain leads to maintain program coherence and avoid fragmentation.
Ideal Candidate Attributes
Thrives in complex environments and provides clarity amidst ambiguity.
Structured, disciplined and proactive in driving program momentum.
Influential communicator who builds strong relationships across diverse stakeholder groups.











Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply. To apply please click apply or call Phillip Yee on +61 2 82893143 for a confidential discussion.

Transformation Business Analyst — Join our Transformation Team
Are you passionate about driving change and turning business needs into practical solutions? We’re seeking a skilled Business Analyst to support transformational initiatives, working closely with stakeholders, technical teams and vendors to deliver high-quality project outcomes.

Lead requirements gathering, analysis and documentation for transformation projects

Create process maps, use cases, functional specifications and other analysis artifacts
Collaborate with technical teams and vendors to ensure requirements are implemented and traceable
Support test planning, test case creation and validation against business requirements

Relevant qualification or equivalent experience in business analysis, IT, or related field

Experience with project tools and productivity software (e.g., Excel, PowerPoint, requirements tools)
Strong verbal, written and presentation skills; confident engaging a diverse range of stakeholders
Proven customer-service focus and ability to build collaborative working relationships
Problem-solving mindset with attention to detail and a drive for continuous improvement
Why apply

Work on impactful transformation initiatives
Collaborate with cross-functional teams and external partners
Opportunities for professional growth and skills development

Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

The team

You will join the Artificial Intelligence Platform Services (AIPS) & Analytics team, comprising over 250 members across the Asia-Pacific region. This team is structured into cross-disciplinary squads, bringing together professionals from Data Science, Product, Engineering, and Data Analysis to collaboratively research, design, build, and support end-to-end AI-based services that deliver direct value to hirers and job seekers. The safe and responsible use of data and AI is a cornerstone of the team's mission, driving efforts to make employment markets fairer, more accessible, transparent, and efficient for everyone.  

To learn more about SEEK’s approach to Responsible AI, please visit SEEK’s Responsible AI page: https://www.seek.com.au/about/responsible-ai

About the role 

This Senior Data Scientist role sits within the Artificial Intelligence Platform Services (AIPS) team and reports to the Head of Responsible AI. You will be a mixture of a Data Scientist, a Machine Learning Engineer, and a Researcher.  Successful performance in this role will be demonstrated by developing insightful research and analyses on AI-based products and proposing and implementing changes to deliver positive social impact. Furthermore, the successful candidate will be expected to stay up to date with emerging techniques and methodologies in Responsible AI, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Machine Learning, and be deeply involved in a culture of continual improvement and collaboration.  Someone passionate about solving practical and impactful challenges in the intersection areas of a multi-disciplinary field to build long-term sustainable, Responsible AI businesses. You will have the opportunity to shape the future of Responsible AI at SEEK by leveraging our governance processes, developing and distributing best practices, and driving innovation. 

Responsibilities 

Partner with squads to embed Responsible AI into AI/ML and analytics product design and delivery.
Design, test, and continuously improve Responsible AI assessment methods, tools, and metrics collaboratively.
Run robust experiments on AI-based services and iterate data products with Strategy, Product, Data Science, and Engineering.
Develop and consolidate Responsible AI guidelines, playbooks, training, and supporting materials.
Support squads in integrating Responsible AI processes into the AI development lifecycle.
Advocate for Responsible AI/ML best practices, clearly communicating benefits and limitations to stakeholders.
Gather, model, and validate relevant data; contribute to research planning, experimental design, and problem-solving across APAC teams.
Qualifications

Skills and experience 

3+ years’ experience building and improving data-informed, internet-scale products.
Postgraduate qualification in a quantitative or behavioural science discipline  (e.g. Physics, Mathematics, Bioinformatics, Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics)
Strong knowledge of Responsible AI/AI Ethics frameworks and AI risk concepts.
Solid foundations in algorithms and data structures, plus ML, IR, recommender systems, LLMs, NLP, or HCI.
Expertise in data analysis, statistics, and experimentation (online and offline).
Proficiency in Python and data visualisation/EDA tools (e.g., Databricks, Tableau).
Strong communication skills, stakeholder collaboration experience, and ability to work independently in interdisciplinary environments.
Additional Information

Permanent Perks
At SEEK we offer:

Annual Performance Bonus Plan
Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
Paid and unpaid leave benefits including Personal Flexi Days and Volunteer Days, as well as the opportunity to purchase additional leave
Support for parents with 14 weeks paid primary carers leave and 2 weeks paid leave for partners
The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

Who are we?

Craft your future with Suntory Oceania, a new $3b multi-beverage partnership between Suntory Global Spirits and Suntory Beverage & Food. Underpinned by quality, craftsmanship and a strong sense of purpose, our exciting new partnership will be powered by two manufacturing sites, five distribution centres and more than 1500 Suntorians across Australia and New Zealand.

Our market-leading portfolio will have over 40 brands, including Jim Beam® and Maker's Mark® Bourbon, Hibiki® Japanese Whisky, Canadian Club® Whisky, -196, V Energy, Maximus, and Suntory BOSS Coffee. As One Suntory, we will be positioned as the fourth largest beverage player in the region, ready to ignite the category, creating more beverage moments from sunrise to sunset. Together, we will bring the best of Suntory to Oceania.




The Opportunity:

As BI Product Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role in our Analytics, AI & Automation (AAA) capability. You’ll own the Analytics Product layer, translating business needs into scalable, governed, and insight-driven solutions built on Azure, Databricks, and consumed through Power BI.

You’ll partner with business Product Owners, Data Engineering, AI, and Automation teams to define and deliver a clear BI product roadmap that lifts analytics maturity and enables trusted insights at scale.




Key Responsibilities:

Shape the BI Strategy: Own and evolve the BI product vision and roadmap aligned to AAA strategy and business priorities.

Deliver Scalable Solutions: Ensure BI products align with Azure and Databricks standards and support advanced analytics use cases.

Stakeholder Engagement: Act as the primary interface between business and technical teams, driving prioritisation and adoption.

Advanced BI Development: Guide the design of high-quality Power BI dashboards and reports, applying strong DAX and SQL skills.

Data Governance: Embed governance, metric consistency, and security into BI products.

Agile Delivery: Manage the BI product backlog and deliver iteratively within Agile frameworks.

Commercial Impact: Ensure BI solutions deliver measurable business value across revenue growth, cost efficiency, and performance.




What you need to be successful:

Expertise in Power BI, including advanced DAX and SQL skills.

Strong understanding of Azure and Databricks platforms.

Proven experience in Agile product management and stakeholder engagement.

A data governance mindset and ability to embed trust into analytics products.

Commercial acumen to translate insights into business outcomes.

Desirable:

Knowledge of data engineering, ETL processes, and semantic modelling.

Proficiency in Python or R for advanced analytics.

Aesthetic sense for creating visually compelling dashboards.

Job Title: Senior Dynamics 365 CE Technical Consultant - Customer Insights
Ad Text:
We are working with a Microsoft consulting practice that is continuing to scale its customer engagement capability and is looking to hire a Senior Technial Consultant specialising in Dynamics 365 Customer Insights and Marketing.

This role offers exposure to a high volume of project work, a mix of established clients and new greenfield implementations, and a clear pathway into leadership as the practice grows.

The Role

You will work across customer data, segmentation, and marketing automation initiatives, partnering closely with clients to design and deliver modern engagement solutions.

Responsibilities will include:

Leading discovery sessions, requirements gathering, and solution design

Designing and delivering segmentation and audience strategies for targeted engagement

Implementing Dynamics 365 Marketing and Real Time Journeys

Advising on consent management, forms, events, and campaign automation

Collaborating with architects and technical teams to ensure data is fit for activation

Running client workshops and acting as a trusted advisor throughout delivery

Supporting a strong pipeline of concurrent projects across multiple industries

About You

This role suits a confident, client-facing consultant who enjoys variety and responsibility.

You may come from a Marketing, CRM, or Customer Insights background and bring:

Strong hands-on experience with Dynamics 365 Marketing and or Customer Insights

Exposure to Real Time Marketing and modern journey design

Some experience working with the Power Platform and Dataverse (deep expertise not required)

Experience delivering in a consulting or agency environment

Strong communication skills and the ability to work autonomously

Ambition to progress into a lead or leadership role over time

Why This Role

Clear and supported pathway into leadership

Consistent project flow with minimal bench time

Opportunity to influence solution design and delivery approach

Collaborative team within a broader Power Platform practice

Location

Sydney preferred

Hybrid working environment

If you're looking for a senior consulting role with real ownership, ongoing project work, and long-term progression, we'd like to speak with you.

The Opportunity

This role is perfect for someone who enjoys being hands‑on and playing a key role in the smooth running of investment governance and supplier management. You’ll be joining a mature, well‑structured environment where frameworks, policies and automation are already established—allowing you to focus on delivery, relationships and continuous improvement.

You’ll thrive here if you learn quickly, take initiative and feel confident engaging with external investment managers, internal stakeholders and contract owners. Strong communication skills, curiosity and the ability to influence across teams will set you up for success. While the technical governance elements can be developed on the job, experience in regulatory risk, compliance, legal or investment operations will help you hit the ground running.

How You'll Make an Impact

Support the upkeep and ongoing enhancement of investment governance processes, policies and dashboards, including contributing to automation initiatives.

Manage day‑to‑day interactions with external investment managers and key investment suppliers.

Assist in maintaining the operational due diligence framework, ensuring documentation and processes remain accurate and up to date.

Contribute to contract updates and IMA maintenance as external investment management services transition into the business.

About You

To be successful in this role, you bring:

Experience in regulatory risk, compliance, legal, or investment operations.

Exposure to insurance, superannuation or funds management (advantageous but not essential).

Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to influence and build productive relationships.

Familiarity with investment or risk policies within an asset‑owner environment.

Industry qualifications such as CFA (or progression toward it) are valued but not required.

This permanent role offers the chance to grow your capability while making a meaningful impact from day one. If you enjoy ownership, relationship‑building and working across policy maintenance, contract updates and supplier oversight, this opportunity provides a strong platform to develop within a collaborative, purpose‑driven investment governance team.

About Us 

Automic Group is Australia’s market leader in technology-driven investor administration. Combining proprietary cloud-native technology with expert service, Automic simplifies investor administration and governance for companies and funds. We provide the market's leading registry, employee share plans, IR analytics, and fund administration services for over 1,400 ASX-listed and unlisted organisations.  

Automic’s culture is collaborative, inclusive, and purpose driven. It is grounded in our mission to connect companies and funds to their investors through technology, service, and trust, and guided by our values of One Automic, Go Beyond, Empowered, and Accountable. 




About the Role 

The Senior Risk Manager is a senior Line 2 leadership role responsible for the continuous refinement and operationalisation of Automic Group’s Risk Management Framework. This position strengthens enterprise and operational risk capability across the Group by embedding disciplined, practical and commercially aligned risk management practices that enable growth. 

You will integrate risk appetite into decision-making, leverage technology and AI to enhance risk insights, and deliver high-quality quantitative and qualitative reporting to Executive and Board stakeholders. Working closely with business leaders, you will ensure risk management supports strategic objectives — enabling calculated risk-taking rather than constraining ambition. 

This is a high-autonomy role operating with CRO oversight and strong executive engagement. 

 

Responsibilities 

Refine and operationalise the Line 2 Risk Management Framework aligned to ISO 31000 

Embed risk appetite considerations into business processes, systems and decision-making 

Design, develop and monitor KRIs aligned to the Risk Appetite Statement 

Facilitate structured quarterly risk reviews and oversee risk registers across business units 

Strengthen operational risk practices including control assessments, incident analysis, BCM and resilience 

Apply quantitative risk methodologies (e.g., Monte Carlo, scenario modelling) to assess material exposures 

Lead implementation and optimisation of an enterprise GRC platform 

Explore and deploy AI-enabled tools for risk identification, analytics and predictive modelling 

Deliver clear, insightful Board and Executive risk reporting and dashboards 

Act as a trusted advisor to business leaders, providing constructive Line 2 challenge 

Promote and uplift risk culture through workshops, education and practical engagement 

 




Skills and Experience 

8+ years’ experience in enterprise and/or operational risk 

Strong understanding of ISO 31000 and the Three Lines Model 

Experience refining and operating a Risk Management Framework 

Demonstrated experience delivering Board and Executive-level risk reporting 

Experience developing and monitoring KRIs 

Experience facilitating structured risk workshops and register reviews 

Exposure to risk quantification techniques (Monte Carlo, scenario modelling) 

Experience implementing or managing a GRC platform 

Advanced Excel and PowerPoint capability 

Strong data analysis and dashboarding capability (e.g., Power BI) 

Commercial acumen with ability to translate risk into business impact 

Desirable: 

Professional certification (RMIA, GAICD, CRISC, FRM, CFA or equivalent) 

Experience in financial services, funds, registry or technology-enabled environments 

Exposure to AI or automation tools in analytics or reporting 

 

About You 

You are a commercially minded risk leader who understands that great risk management enables growth. You combine analytical rigour with pragmatic execution and are comfortable operating independently in a fast-paced, evolving environment. 

To be successful in this role, we are looking for someone to bring: 

Strong analytical and critical thinking capability 

Confidence engaging and influencing senior stakeholders 

The ability to challenge constructively while building trusted partnerships 

Clear and compelling written and verbal communication skills 

A continuous improvement mindset 

High attention to detail with strong delivery discipline 

Curiosity and enthusiasm for leveraging technology and AI in risk management 

Sound judgement in prioritising risk treatments and strategic trade-offs 

The ability to balance governance discipline with business enablement 

 

Why join Automic: 

You’ll be part of a team where operational excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement are genuinely valued. This is an opportunity to deepen your expertise, contribute to meaningful outcomes, and grow your career within a high-performing, purpose-led fintech environment. 

Key Benefits include: 

Hybrid working  

A High growth, scale-up organisation 

Professional career growth & development  

Supportive & fast paced environment 

 

If the above sounds like you, please apply for this exciting opportunity and be part of the growth journey at Automic! 

 

Applications will be reviewed as they are received and interviews may commence prior to the closing date.

Job Description


About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

What you’ll do

Lead and grow a high-performing team of Machine Learning Engineers focused on automating content review and moderation across Canva’s Content Library.

Set and execute the technical vision and strategy for ML-driven safety, quality, and policy enforcement across text, image, video, audio and template content.

Design and scale robust ML systems — including experimentation frameworks, evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and feedback loops to continuously improve model performance and close the gap between manual review and automation.

Partner cross-functionally with Content, Product, Review Ops, and Review Engineering to deliver impactful, user-facing solutions and scalable human-in-the-loop systems.

Drive large, end-to-end initiatives from problem framing and roadmap definition through to delivery, experimentation, rollout, and impact measurement.

Uplift engineering excellence through strong technical direction, pragmatic architecture decisions, and a healthy code review and knowledge-sharing culture.

Coach and mentor engineers to navigate complex technical challenges and do the best work of their lives.

Champion operational excellence, responsible AI, and safety-by-design, ensuring our systems serve all users, including students and educators, with quality, integrity, and trust.

Stay ahead of industry trends and emerging ML best practices, continuously evolving the domain to meet Canva’s scale and ambition.

You're probably a match if

You’re passionate about coaching and developing engineers, creating a culture of growth and support.

You’re experienced in designing systems at scale, with a focus on reliability, performance, and extensibility.

You collaborate cross-functionally and thrive in partnerships with product, ML, and content stakeholders.

You think strategically and deliver pragmatically, balancing long-term vision with immediate needs.

You value diversity, empathy, and inclusion, and lead in a way that fosters belonging and trust.

You have experience leading an engineering team in a Product or SaaS environment, ideally within ML or applied AI domains.

Prior experience in text, image, video, and audio understanding is highly desirable.

About us 
We are an award winning, national $16B public offer industry fund focused on the education and community sectors. Working for NGS Super means being part of something bigger and working to make a difference to our members and their financial future.  

Our people are key to our success, and as we expand, we’re committed to finding and retaining the right talent to take on the journey.  As well as a flexible and fun workplace, we offer competitive benefits including additional leave entitlements, personal & professional development and health & wellbeing programs. 

Click on the video below to learn more! 

The Role 
Working closely with the Senior Manager, Responsible Investment, you’ll deliver high quality ESG research, analysis, reporting and engagement to support informed investment decisions.
The role spans policy and ESG strategy, research, investment selection and monitoring, and active ownership—requiring strong technical expertise, analytical insight and hands on execution to advance the Fund’s responsible investment objectives.

What you’ll do

Policy and Strategy: Support the maintenance and enhancement of the Fund’s responsible investment framework, policies and procedures. Ensure team awareness of policy updates. Undertake research to identify emerging ESG issues, regulatory changes and industry best practice, and provide recommendations to the Senior Manager. 
Research & Implementation: Conduct research on ESG themes, sector-level trends, controversies, standards and best practice to inform decision making. Prepare well structured ESG thematic papers, manager briefing notes, and Investment Committee inputs.  Support monitoring of the investment portfolio for ESG risks and opportunities, including assessment of data platforms, external ratings and stewardship inputs. 
Selection, Monitoring and Reporting: Complete ESG due diligence on investment opportunities, including data gathering, scoring, documentation and preparation of reports. Support the ESG compliance review process and summarise findings for internal stakeholders. Prepare internal ESG dashboards, metrics and reporting, ensuring data integrity and alignment with governance requirements. 
Engagement: Support internal and external engagement activities, including active ownership, stewardship, and proxy voting. Assist with the monitoring of proxy voting outcomes and prepare summaries for the Senior Manager. Coordinate engagement actions with external managers and stewardship partners, tracking progress and outcomes. 
Cross-functional ESG initiatives: Provide analytical and coordination support for organisation wide ESG initiatives (e.g., AASB S2, climate strategy, modern slavery, biodiversity etc). Collaborate as needed with other teams to ensure ESG related workstreams are delivered on time and to required standards. 

About you 
With a strong understanding of responsible investment and ESG integration concepts across asset classes, you will bring the following key skills:

Demonstrated analytical capability, including ability to interpret ESG data, identify issues, and prepare high quality written outputs. 
Working knowledge of ESG standards and frameworks (e.g., PRI, TCFD/IFRS S2, SASB, GHG Protocol). 
Experience contributing to ESG processes such as due diligence, monitoring, reporting or engagement. 
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to translate analysis into clear insights. 
Strong stakeholder collaboration skills. 
Qualifications at an appropriate level in a relevant discipline (e.g., Finance, Economics, Law, Sustainability, Commerce) or demonstrated equivalent experience.

The following skills are nice to have but not essential:

Experience in climate risk analysis, scenario modelling or portfolio level climate assessments. 
Familiarity with biodiversity and nature related risks. 
Experience supporting ESG disclosures to Boards, regulators or members. 
Experience with ESG data platforms, ratings providers or analytical tools. 
Experience within a superannuation fund, institutional investor or asset manager. 
Relevant postgraduate study or professional certification (e.g., CFA, ESG certifications).

If you have high levels of professional judgement, integrity and accountability, with a strong member-first mindset, would love to hear from you.

We are a super fund that has an exceptional work culture, provides a diverse offering in developing our people and you can be a part of it while earning an attractive remuneration package! 

Interested and suitably qualified candidates should submit a copy of their updated CV. 

Please note that to be eligible for this role, you are required to have permanent Australian working rights and residency. 

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to creating a workplace that values diversity, equity, and respect for all individuals.

About the team

Expect to face intellectual challenges daily, as you delve into intricate policy issues and analyse information to provide valuable insights.

Given the ever-evolving nature of geopolitics, you can expect a fast-paced work environment. That means staying up to date with current events and adapting to new changes quickly.

The team values collaboration. You’ll engage in lively discussions, brainstorming sessions, and knowledge sharing.

The roles

ONI is recruiting for multiple current and possible future analyst vacancies at the EL2 classification in our Intelligence Assessments Divisions.

Our Intelligence Analysts apply high level analytical tradecraft and research acumen to generate assessments and briefings on matters of political, strategic and economic significance to Australia. Our analysts help inform national security decision making by providing the Prime Minister and other members of the National Security Committee of Cabinet with intelligence-based advice, analysis and assessments.

Our analysts specialise in delivering insight by rigorously synthesising, distilling and analysing information to meet our customers’ needs. We draw on all sources of information available to the Australian Government including material covertly collected by other intelligence agencies, diplomatic reporting, open-source intelligence, and insights we gather from talking to world-leading experts in business, academia and think-tanks.

As an EL2 team member you will:

Perform an important leadership role, developing and delivering strategic, policies, priorities and operational practices in support of ONI’s objectives.
Plan, develop, edit and deliver strategic intelligence products that ensure ONI has policy impact, through analytical rigour, effective prioritisation and sound business processes.
Deliver specialist briefings to senior stakeholders and provide timely input to policy processes.
Cultivate, establish and maintain strong working relationships with a diverse range of stakeholders across government, with external experts, and with foreign partners.
Represent and negotiate highly complex issues on behalf of ONI at, a variety of internal and external meetings and working groups.
Foster ONI’s analytical principles and standards, and  provide direction to develop staff and analytical capability.
Contribute to ONI’s leadership of the National Intelligence Community.
Who are we looking for?

We recruit staff with a wide range of backgrounds, skill levels, disciplines and working styles. We look for aptitude and insight, rather than time served in similar roles. We welcome applications from individuals at all career stages and from any sector. A commonality all our analysts have is a deep interest in helping to protect people and places.

We have a specific interest in candidates with backgrounds in the following areas:

Thematic – Economics, Trade, Climate and Energy, Terrorism, Transnational Crime, Irregular Migration, Military Capability, Major Power Dynamics, Strategic Outlook, Foreign Interference.
Geopolitical – Pacific, Southeast Asia, China, Europe, Africa, Middle East, India, Japan/Koreas, America.
Critical Technology and Cyber – including Artificial Intelligence, quantum, biotech and space.

Your experience and knowledge may have been developed while working for the Australian government in a diplomatic, policy or intelligence role. Or you may have a think tank, academic, private sector or other relevant background. 

At the EL2 classification we look for deep and broad subject matter expertise, a strong ability to exercise astute decision making, and an aptitude for coordinating and collaborating with other NIC agencies. Our EL2s also play a strong role in building organisational capability and exercising leadership. For more information on the APS Work Level Standards, please visit the APSC website.

Key attributes

To be successful in this role you will need to demonstrate:

Experience in a leadership role, with the ability to develop analytical capability.
Advanced subject matter knowledge with a well-developed understanding of the strategic implications for Australia.
Demonstrated experience in integrated analysis, evaluating contested information and reaching robust conclusions.
Advanced written and verbal communication skills with the ability to communicate clearly and persuasively.
Excellent interpersonal and representational skills, with the ability to build strong internal and external relationships and to represent ONI in the context of highly complex and sensitive issues.
Proven capacity to manage collaborative projects, set priorities and evaluate outcomes, both internally and with other agencies.

Desirable:

Demonstrated ability to set the strategic direction for a team whilst actively managing resources and staff.

For further information about these opportunities and how to apply, please download a candidate information kit from the careers page of ONI's website.

Job Title: Senior Data Scientist - ML and AI
Ad Text:
This is: the job
You’ll join a high-performing Data Science team at the centre of a growing AI and machine learning capability within a large, enterprise environment. This is a 12 month contract based in Melbourne, focused on delivering advanced analytics and production grade Machine Learning solutions that drive measurable commercial impact.
As the Senior Data Scientist, you’ll take ownership of designing, building, and deploying scalable machine learning models using Azure Machine Learning. You’ll operate across the full ML lifecycle, from proof of concept through to production, ensuring solutions are robust, reliable, and aligned to business outcomes. Success in this role means delivering models that don’t just work in theory but create real value in-market.

This is: what you’ll do
Partner with stakeholders to identify high-value AI/ML use cases and define measurable success metrics
Design, develop, and deploy end to end machine learning solutions using Azure ML
Lead rapid proof of concept development to validate feasibility and business impact
Own feature engineering, model selection, hyperparameter tuning, validation, and performance optimisation
Implement and enhance MLOps practices, including CI/CD, automated monitoring, drift detection, and retraining
Build and maintain scalable ML pipelines for batch and real time inference via Azure ML Endpoints
Collaborate with data engineers and software engineers to integrate feature stores and production grade data pipelines
Lead code reviews and contribute to solution design workshops to uplift technical standards
Present insights, demos, and recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Mentor and support other data scientists, fostering a strong culture of learning and technical excellence

This is: what you’ll need
5+ years’ hands-on experience delivering ML models from development through to production
Strong expertise in Python and SQL (R or other languages for experimentation advantageous)
Deep experience with Azure Machine Learning, Pipelines, Data Factory, and MLOps frameworks
Proven capability across feature engineering, model evaluation, optimisation, and lifecycle management
Experience implementing CI/CD for ML, automated retraining strategies, and cloud-based ML infrastructure
Demonstrated success delivering and iterating on proof-of-concepts in fast-paced environments
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to translate complex analytics into business value
Exposure to recommendation systems, time series forecasting, or geospatial analytics (desirable)

This is: the perks
Work on large-scale, enterprise AI initiatives with regional impact
Influence architecture and ML standards across multiple business domains
Access to cutting-edge tooling and high-performance hardware

This is: for good
Apply with purpose. Be part of a placement that gives back. For every role we fill, we donate to a charity of the client’s choice. We’ve surpassed $1 million donated and we’re just getting started.

This is: what’s next
Apply now or reach out to:
Leanne O’Connor
Director, Natural Selection Group

Job Title: Senior Financial Analyst - Controlling (18 month Fixed Term Contract)
Ad Text:
About Miele 

What does it take for a family-run company to become world market leader in the premium domestic appliance segment? It takes more than offering average products, run-of-the-mill service or short-sighted actions. It also requires the aspiration to do it forever better and to be forever better. Our colleagues can rest assured that those who live up to our standards of quality and professionalism grow personally and professionally. No matter if they are apprentices or managers. 

Make our claim your challenge - whether in Gütersloh, Melbourne or Singapore! Become one of more than 23,000 Miele employees in 50 countries around the world who are committed to making our products even better every day - and help us to expand our innovation leadership.

About Your Workplace

Reporting to the Head of Controlling, this Senior Financial Analyst role is a key strategic partner within the organisation, providing insightful analysis and performance reporting that supports decision-making and drives sustainable growth across Miele Australia.

Your Responsibilities

• Lead budgeting and forecasting processes for Marketing and Product, including preparation and submission of corporate templates.
• Deliver scenario modelling for new product introductions, promotions, and commission structures to optimise returns.
• Oversee month-end close activities for Marketing and Product, ensuring accuracy and integrity of results.
• Maintain and enhance reporting tools (Cognos, Data Warehouse, Power BI), ensuring data accuracy and delivering meaningful performance dashboards.

What We Are Looking For

Must-have:
• CA or CPA qualification with strong management accounting and financial analysis capability
• Advanced analytical and financial modelling skills with the ability to translate data into commercial insights
• Strong ERP and business systems experience (SAP, AS400, Cognos or similar)
• Proven ability to partner with stakeholders and influence decision-making across functions
• Excellent Excel and reporting skills with strong attention to detail and reconciliation capability

Nice-to-have:
• Experience in Retail, Distribution or FMCG environments
• Exposure to Power BI, Business Objects or other data visualisation tools
• Experience driving process improvements and acting as a change agent
• Strong understanding of profitability analysis across product portfolios

Our Benefits

• Flexible working hours
• Personal development and career opportunities
• International development programs
• Access to a wide range of wellbeing programs, including annual flu vaccinations and employee assistance support
• Experience meaningful recognition for both individual accomplishments and team successes
